title: Agent Execution Loop
activity_flow: START: caller invokes agent(instructions, messages, tools, ?schema)
DECISION: schema provided? → YES → new StructuredAnonymousAgent | NO → new AnonymousAgent
AnonymousAgent constructed (implements Agent, Conversational, HasTools, uses Promptable trait)
Caller invokes agent->prompt(prompt, attachments, ?provider, ?model, ?timeout)
Promptable::prompt() → calls withModelFailover(callback, provider, model)
withModelFailover: resolve provider list via getProvidersAndModels() (checks method/attribute, falls back to config('ai.default'))
LOOP: foreach provider/model pair (failover loop)
→ Ai::textProviderFor() resolves Provider instance
→ getDefaultModelFor() reads UseSmartestModel / UseCheapestModel / default
→ Provider->prompt(AgentPrompt) called inside try/catch(FailoverableException)
→ DECISION: FailoverableException thrown? YES → dispatch AgentFailedOver, continue loop | NO → return result
Provider::prompt() (GeneratesText trait):
1. Generate invocationId (uuid7)
2. Build middleware pipeline: gatherMiddlewareFor(agent) → check RemembersConversations, HasMiddleware
3. pipeline()->send(prompt)->through(middleware)->then(closure)
Inside pipeline closure:
a. dispatch PromptingAgent event
b. Collect messages: agent->messages() if Conversational, append new UserMessage(prompt, attachments)
c. listenForToolInvocations(): register onToolInvocation callbacks on gateway (dispatches InvokingTool / ToolInvoked events)
d. Detect schema: agent instanceof HasStructuredOutput → agent->schema(JsonSchemaTypeFactory)
e. textGateway()->generateText(provider, model, instructions, messages, tools, schema, options, timeout)
f. DECISION: structured? → wrap in StructuredAgentResponse | else AgentResponse (attach toolCalls, toolResults, steps)
4. dispatch AgentPrompted event
5. return AgentResponse
Gateway::generateText() (e.g. OpenAiGateway):
1. buildTextRequestBody(provider, model, instructions, messages, tools, schema, options)
2. HTTP POST to provider API ('responses' endpoint)
3. validateTextResponse(data) — throws AiException on error
4. parseTextResponse(data, ...) → delegates to processResponse()
processResponse() — recursive tool loop:
1. Extract: text, toolCalls, reasonings, citations, usage, finishReason from response output
2. mapToolCallsWithReasoning → array<ToolCall(id, name, arguments, resultId, reasoningId, reasoningSummary)>
3. Create Step(text, toolCalls, [], finishReason, usage, meta) and push to $steps collection
4. Append AssistantMessage to $messages
DECISION: finishReason == ToolCalls AND toolCalls not empty AND steps < maxSteps (default: round(toolCount * 1.5))?
YES (tool calls needed):
a. executeToolCalls(): foreach ToolCall → findTool(name, tools) → executeTool(tool, arguments)
b. executeTool(): dispatch invokingToolCallback → tool->handle(new Request(arguments)) → dispatch toolInvokedCallback → return string result
c. Build array<ToolResult(id, name, arguments, result, resultId)>
d. Update Step in $steps with toolResults
e. Append ToolResultMessage to $messages
f. continueWithToolResults(): POST follow-up request with previous_response_id + tool results input
g. validateTextResponse() on follow-up
h. RECURSE: processResponse(newData, ..., depth+1) → back to step 1 of this block
NO (stop condition reached — FinishReason::Stop / Length / Error / maxSteps exceeded):
a. Aggregate allToolCalls and allToolResults from all steps
b. DECISION: structured? → return StructuredTextResponse(structuredData, text, combinedUsage, meta) | else TextResponse(text, usage, meta)
c. Attach .withMessages(messages).withSteps(steps).withToolCallsAndResults()
END: AgentResponse (or StructuredAgentResponse) returned to caller
key_conditions: agent instanceof Conversational → prepend historical messages
agent instanceof HasTools → pass tools to gateway
agent instanceof HasStructuredOutput → pass JSON schema to gateway
agent instanceof HasMiddleware → add middleware to pipeline
RemembersConversations trait present AND hasConversationParticipant() → add RememberConversation middleware
finishReason == ToolCalls AND steps.count < maxSteps → continue tool loop
FailoverableException caught → try next provider in failover list

key_data_objects.AgentPrompt: agent, prompt:string, attachments:Collection, provider:TextProvider, model:string, timeout:?int
key_data_objects.ToolCall: id:string, name:string, arguments:array, resultId:?string, reasoningId:?string, reasoningSummary:?array
key_data_objects.Step: text:string, toolCalls:array<ToolCall>, toolResults:array<ToolResult>, finishReason:FinishReason, usage:Usage, meta:Meta
key_data_objects.AgentResponse: invocationId:string, text:string, usage:Usage, meta:Meta + toolCalls, toolResults, steps, messages

key_relationships: AnonymousAgent implements Agent + Conversational + HasTools, uses Promptable trait
Promptable::prompt() delegates to Provider via withModelFailover()
Provider uses GeneratesText trait which owns the pipeline + listenForToolInvocations()
Provider delegates actual HTTP call to TextGateway (OpenAiGateway, AnthropicGateway, etc.)
InvokesTools::executeTool() calls Tool->handle(Request) and fires callbacks
processResponse() is recursive: calls continueWithToolResults() → HTTP POST → processResponse()
important_notes: maxSteps defaults to round(toolCount * 1.5) if not explicitly set via TextGenerationOptions
Tool loop is recursive via processResponse() calling continueWithToolResults() which POSTs follow-up with previous_response_id
Events fired: PromptingAgent (before), AgentPrompted (after), InvokingTool, ToolInvoked, AgentFailedOver
Middleware pipeline runs on AgentPrompt before gateway call — supports conversation memory, custom middleware
Each provider driver (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Groq, etc.) has its own ParsesTextResponses and HandlesTextStreaming but shares the same structural loop pattern
StructuredAnonymousAgent variant parses final text as JSON and returns StructuredAgentResponse
